reasoning:
Historically, in any 5-day trading week (like March 13 vs March 20 since March 15/22 are Sundays) a typical tech stock has around a 53-54% chance of going up simply due to the long-term equity premium drift. The yfinance tool gives an anchor estimate of 0.57 for this specific stock and season. However, AXON's fundamental valuation is extremely rich (P/E of over 340, PEG around 12) and the stock has run into significant momentum headwinds recently due to insider selling and a realization of profitability challenges despite 30%+ revenue growth. Last week the stock declined significantly (13%).

Weakness over an earnings period usually persists, but here their Q4 earnings were actually reported on Feb 24 and the stock spiked 18%. The subsequent drop (down to ~496 by March 13) erased most of that post-earnings run. While Mean Reversion might be expected, insider selling during a downward trend and "tension between revenue gains and margin pressures" is a bearish setup. Therefore I am penalizing the historical baseline of ~54% down to just below 50% given the extreme recent downward momentum (every single day in the last 4 days was down) and negative fundamental narrative (insider selling, margin concerns) in the tech sector at this moment.
